_comment: MIT pavlab lab-fleet registry for the shoreside connectivity collector. Same internal address plan as Greece (eth0 gw = 10.<id>.1.1, backseat = 10.<id>.1.100, both derived from BOAT_ID), BUT the lab shore uplink does NOT follow the Greece 10.1.0.<id> formula -- it is 192.168.1.(100+id) on the pavlab 192.168.1.0/24 backbone. Each boat therefore pins its shore presence with an explicit 'uplink' override; 'frontseat' and 'backseat' still derive from 'id'. Run with: ./collect.py --config fleet.mit.json. Source: Blue Boat lab network documentation (March 2026).
_comment_radio: Subsystem C: RF/mesh from the shared lab DoodleLabs shoreside radio at 192.168.1.130 (the Linksys pavlab router is 192.168.1.1). 'macs' maps each boat name to its boat-radio MAC (lowercase, colon-separated); blanks mean 'not yet known' and surface under the dashboard 'unmapped' list. Credentials default to the June-2024+ firmware (user/DoodleSmartRadio).
